recap: Switzerland advanced to the round of 16 with a dominant second-half performance, as Breel Embolo opened the scoring early in the first half before Dan Ndoye's powerful strike from the top of the box just after halftime sealed a 2-0 victory over Algeria. Though Algeria controlled the opening minutes, Switzerland's clinical finishing and goalkeeper work prevented any dramatic comeback, with Fabian Rieder nearly adding a third late in the match. The Swiss will face the Colombia-Ghana winner in Vancouver next Tuesday, seeking to end a 72-year drought in World Cup knockout matches.
